summary: DHH shares a brief recommendation of blogger Michael Barrish, praising his ability to describe emotional situations with raw authenticity. He highlights a passage from Barrish's piece 'Container' on oblivio.com as an example of writing that captures post-rationalism in real time. The post is a simple link-blog entry, typical of early 2000s blogging culture, where sharing and curating other writers' work was a core practice.
key_points: DHH curates and recommends other bloggers' writing, highlighting Michael Barrish as a standout voice
He values writing that captures raw emotional intensity and stream-of-consciousness authenticity
The post reflects early blogging culture's emphasis on link-sharing and community curation
money_quotes: Michael Barrish describes emotional situations like no other blogger on my daily route.
The writing flows naturally, and you get the post-rationalism real time.
key_insight: Great writing captures the raw, unprocessed intensity of emotional experience rather than polished retrospective analysis.
